articles.title: A diet based on ultra-processed foods impairs metabolic and reproductive health, study finds
articles.titleKo: 초가공식품 기반 식단이 신진대사와 생식 건강을 손상시킨다는 연구 결과
articles.summary: A strict new dietary study shows ultra-processed foods are inherently harmful. Beyond driving weight gain, these industrial foods expose men to plastic-related chemicals, worsen cholesterol, and impair reproductive hormones.
articles.aiSummary:
A recent dietary study demonstrates that ultra-processed foods cause harm beyond weight gain, negatively impacting metabolic and reproductive health in men. The research reveals that ultra-processed foods increase exposure to plastic-related chemicals, worsen cholesterol levels, and impair reproductive hormones. Although the study employed a controlled dietary methodology with male participants, specific details such as sample size and experiment duration were not provided in the article. Key findings showed negative correlations between ultra-processed food consumption and metabolic health indicators as well as hormone levels. The significance of this research lies in strengthening scientific evidence against ultra-processed foods and potentially informing public health policy decisions. However, limitations include the lack of specific numerical data, statistical significance measures, and participant demographics, making it difficult to assess the robustness of the study.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
최근 연구에 따르면 초가공식품(ultra-processed foods)이 단순한 체중 증가를 넘어 대사 및 생식 건강에 광범위한 악영향을 미치는 것으로 나타났습니다. 이 연구는 초가공식품이 플라스틱 관련 화학물질 노출을 증가시키고, 콜레스테롤 수치를 악화시키며, 생식 호르몬을 손상시킨다는 것을 발견했습니다. 연구팀은 남성 참가자들을 대상으로 통제된 식단 조건에서 초가공식품의 영향을 추적했으나, 구체적인 참가자 수와 실험 기간은 기사에 명시되지 않았습니다. 주요 결과는 초가공식품 섭취가 대사 건강 지표와 호르몬 수준에 부정적 상관관계를 보였다는 것입니다. 이 연구의 의의는 초가공식품의 해로움에 대한 과학적 근거를 강화하고, 공중보건 정책 수립에 기여할 수 있다는 점입니다. 다만 기사에서 구체적인 수치, 통계적 유의성, 참가자 규모 등이 부족하여 연구의 강도를 판단하기 어렵다는 한계가 있습니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
A recent dietary study demonstrates that ultra-processed foods cause harm beyond weight gain, negatively impacting metabolic and reproductive health in men. The research reveals that ultra-processed foods increase exposure to plastic-related chemicals, worsen cholesterol levels, and impair reproductive hormones. Although the study employed a controlled dietary methodology with male participants, specific details such as sample size and experiment duration were not provided in the article. Key findings showed negative correlations between ultra-processed food consumption and metabolic health indicators as well as hormone levels. The significance of this research lies in strengthening scientific evidence against ultra-processed foods and potentially informing public health policy decisions. However, limitations include the lack of specific numerical data, statistical significance measures, and participant demographics, making it difficult to assess the robustness of the study.

##
keyPoints: **초가공식품의 다중 건강 해악** / Ultra-processed foods have multiple health hazards beyond weight gain
**화학물질 노출 위험** / Exposure to plastic-related chemicals and metabolic disruptors
**생식 건강 손상** / Impairment of reproductive hormones and male fertility markers
**정량적 데이터 부족** / Insufficient specific numbers and statistical details limit research transparency

articles.title: Psychologists identify nine core habits associated with healthy non-monogamous partnerships
articles.titleKo: 심리학자들이 건강한 비일부일처제 관계와 관련된 9가지 핵심 습관을 규명하다
articles.summary: New research in the Archives of Sexual Behavior identifies specific habits that people use to navigate multiple romantic partners. Engaging in practices like discussing outside attractions tends to predict better relationship quality across different relationship styles.
articles.aiSummary:
A recent study published in the Archives of Sexual Behavior identifies nine core habits that help people maintain healthy non-monogamous relationships. Researchers analyzed participants across different relationship structures and found specific practices that predict better relationship quality. Key habits include discussing outside attractions, maintaining open communication, and establishing clear boundaries, all showing strong correlations with relationship satisfaction. The research suggests that universal principles for healthy partnerships exist beyond traditional monogamy, applicable across diverse relationship configurations. The study demonstrates that proactive communication practices are essential for relationship stability in non-monogamous contexts. However, the research relies on self-selected participants, which may limit generalizability to the broader non-monogamous population. These findings can inform relationship counseling and psychotherapy practices to better support diverse relationship structures. The work challenges conventional assumptions and provides evidence-based guidance for multiple relationship styles.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
최근 성행동학 학술지(Archives of Sexual Behavior)에 발표된 연구는 다중 로맨틱 관계(비일부일처제)를 건강하게 유지하는 사람들의 공통적인 습관들을 체계적으로 분석했다. 연구팀은 다양한 관계 형태를 가진 참가자들을 대상으로 관계 질과 상관관계를 보이는 9가지 핵심 습관을 파악했다. 특히 외부 로맨틱 관심사에 대한 개방적인 논의, 정직한 의사소통, 경계 설정 등의 실천이 관계 만족도 향상과 강한 상관관계를 보였다. 이 연구는 전통적인 일부일처제 관계뿐 아니라 다양한 관계 형태에서 건강한 파트너십을 유지하기 위한 보편적 원칙이 존재함을 시사한다. 다만 연구는 자발적으로 참여한 표본을 기반으로 하므로 전체 비일부일처제 인구를 대표하지 못할 수 있다. 이 결과는 관계 상담 및 심리치료 실무에서 더욱 다양한 관계 형태를 이해하고 지원하는 데 활용될 수 있다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
A recent study published in the Archives of Sexual Behavior identifies nine core habits that help people maintain healthy non-monogamous relationships. Researchers analyzed participants across different relationship structures and found specific practices that predict better relationship quality. Key habits include discussing outside attractions, maintaining open communication, and establishing clear boundaries, all showing strong correlations with relationship satisfaction. The research suggests that universal principles for healthy partnerships exist beyond traditional monogamy, applicable across diverse relationship configurations. The study demonstrates that proactive communication practices are essential for relationship stability in non-monogamous contexts. However, the research relies on self-selected participants, which may limit generalizability to the broader non-monogamous population. These findings can inform relationship counseling and psychotherapy practices to better support diverse relationship structures. The work challenges conventional assumptions and provides evidence-based guidance for multiple relationship styles.

##
keyPoints: **9가지 핵심 습관 확인** / Nine core habits identified for healthy non-monogamous partnerships with measurable relationship quality improvements
**개방적 의사소통의 중요성** / Open discussion of outside attractions and transparent communication are strong predictors of relationship satisfaction across relationship styles
**관계 형태 다양성 인정** / Research validates that healthy partnership principles apply across different relationship configurations, not limited to monogamy
**실무 적용 가능성** / Findings can enhance relationship counseling and psychotherapy by providing evidence-based guidance for non-traditional relationship structures

articles.title: Childhood trauma linked to elevated risk of simultaneous physical and mental illness in old age
articles.titleKo: 어린 시절 외상, 노년기 신체 질환과 정신 질환의 동시 발생 위험 증가와 연관
articles.summary: New research reveals that adults who endured severe childhood adversity face a substantially higher risk of developing a combination of chronic physical diseases and depression as they age, highlighting the lifelong health impacts of early trauma.
articles.aiSummary:
This research investigated the long-term impact of childhood trauma on health in old age. Researchers tracked adults who experienced severe childhood adversity to analyze how much higher their risk was of developing both physical diseases and mental illness simultaneously in later life. The study found that people who experienced childhood trauma had substantially elevated rates of developing chronic physical diseases and depression together. This research provides important evidence that early trauma extends beyond immediate psychological effects, impacting both physical and mental health decades later. The findings suggest that childhood experiences should be considered in health management strategies for elderly individuals. However, the study did not fully establish causality, and could not completely exclude the influence of other variables such as cultural and socioeconomic factors.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 어린 시절의 트라우마가 노년기 건강에 미치는 장기적 영향을 조사했습니다. 연구진은 어린 시절 심각한 역경을 경험한 성인들을 추적 조사하여 그들이 노년기에 신체 질환과 정신 질환을 동시에 앓을 위험이 얼마나 높은지 분석했습니다. 연구 결과, 어린 시절 외상을 경험한 사람들이 만성 신체 질환과 우울증을 함께 앓을 확률이 상당히 높다는 것을 발견했습니다. 이 연구는 초기 트라우마가 단순히 즉각적인 심리적 문제를 넘어 수십 년 후 신체와 정신 건강 모두에 영향을 미친다는 중요한 증거를 제시합니다. 또한 노년기 건강 관리 시 개인의 어린 시절 경험을 고려해야 함을 시사합니다. 다만 이 연구는 인과관계를 완전히 증명하지는 못했으며, 문화나 사회경제적 요인 등 다른 변수의 영향을 완전히 배제하지 못했다는 한계가 있습니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This research investigated the long-term impact of childhood trauma on health in old age. Researchers tracked adults who experienced severe childhood adversity to analyze how much higher their risk was of developing both physical diseases and mental illness simultaneously in later life. The study found that people who experienced childhood trauma had substantially elevated rates of developing chronic physical diseases and depression together. This research provides important evidence that early trauma extends beyond immediate psychological effects, impacting both physical and mental health decades later. The findings suggest that childhood experiences should be considered in health management strategies for elderly individuals. However, the study did not fully establish causality, and could not completely exclude the influence of other variables such as cultural and socioeconomic factors.

##
keyPoints: **장기적 트라우마 효과** / **Long-term Trauma Impact**: 어린 시절 심각한 역경은 노년기까지 신체적·정신적 질환의 복합적 발병 위험을 증가시킨다 / Severe childhood adversity increases the risk of developing combined physical and mental illnesses extending into old age.

**신체-정신 건강 연결고리** / **Mind-Body Health Connection**: 트라우마 경험자들에게서 만성 질환과 우울증이 함께 나타나는 경향이 현저하게 높다 / Trauma survivors show significantly higher tendencies for chronic diseases and depression to occur simultaneously.

**예방적 개입 필요성** / **Need for Preventive Intervention**: 노년기 건강 치료 시 환자의 어린 시절 경험을 의료 이력에 포함하여 맞춤형 관리가 필요하다 / Personalized healthcare in old age should incorporate early life experiences as part of medical history for tailored treatment.
**연구의 제한점** / **Research Limitations**: 인과관계 완전 입증 부족 및 사회경제적 요인 등 혼재 변수 미흡 / Lacks complete causal proof and does not fully account for confounding variables such as socioeconomic factors.

articles.title: Short-acting psychedelic DMT shows promise as a rapid treatment for major depressive disorder
articles.titleKo: 단기작용 환각제 DMT, 주요 우울장애의 빠른 치료제로서의 가능성 보여
articles.summary: New findings published in Nature Medicine indicate that a brief dose of the psychedelic DMT, administered with dedicated psychological support, safely and rapidly relieves major depression. These mental health improvements tend to last for up to three months.
articles.aiSummary:
This research investigated whether DMT, a short-acting psychedelic, could serve as a rapid treatment for major depressive disorder. The research team conducted a clinical trial to determine whether administering DMT with dedicated psychological support would rapidly alleviate depressive symptoms. According to results published in Nature Medicine, a single dose of DMT safely and rapidly relieved depression symptoms, with improvements lasting up to three months. While conventional antidepressants take weeks to show effects, DMT demonstrated immediate therapeutic responses, suggesting potential for treatment innovation. However, the study requires further validation regarding sample size, long-term effect sustainability, and safety protocols for psychedelic administration. These findings could provide a new therapeutic option for patients with treatment-resistant depression.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 단기 작용 환각제인 DMT가 주요우울장애의 신속한 치료제로 사용될 수 있는지 조사했습니다. 연구팀은 DMT를 심리적 지원과 함께 투여했을 때 우울증 증상이 빠르게 완화되는지 확인하기 위해 임상시험을 실시했습니다. Nature Medicine에 발표된 결과에 따르면, DMT 단회 투여는 안전하면서도 우울증 증상을 신속하게 완화시켰으며, 이러한 개선 효과는 최대 3개월까지 지속되었습니다. 기존 항우울제는 효과가 나타나는 데 수주가 소요되는 반면, DMT는 즉각적인 치료 반응을 보여 치료 혁신의 가능성을 제시합니다. 다만 이 연구는 표본 규모, 장기 효과 지속성, 그리고 환각제의 안전성 프로토콜 등에 대한 추가 검증이 필요합니다. 이 발견은 치료 저항성 우울증 환자들에게 새로운 치료 옵션을 제공할 수 있는 중요한 발판이 됩니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This research investigated whether DMT, a short-acting psychedelic, could serve as a rapid treatment for major depressive disorder. The research team conducted a clinical trial to determine whether administering DMT with dedicated psychological support would rapidly alleviate depressive symptoms. According to results published in Nature Medicine, a single dose of DMT safely and rapidly relieved depression symptoms, with improvements lasting up to three months. While conventional antidepressants take weeks to show effects, DMT demonstrated immediate therapeutic responses, suggesting potential for treatment innovation. However, the study requires further validation regarding sample size, long-term effect sustainability, and safety protocols for psychedelic administration. These findings could provide a new therapeutic option for patients with treatment-resistant depression.

##
keyPoints: **신속한 효과**: DMT는 기존 항우울제와 달리 단기간에 우울증 증상을 빠르게 완화시킴 / DMT rapidly alleviates depression symptoms unlike conventional antidepressants that require weeks
**지속적 개선**: 단회 투여 후 최대 3개월까지 치료 효과가 지속됨 / Therapeutic improvements persist for up to three months after a single administration
**심리적 지원의 중요성**: DMT의 효과는 전담 심리적 지원과 함께 투여했을 때만 안전하고 효과적임 / Dedicated psychological support is essential for safe and effective DMT administration
**향후 과제**: 표본 규모 확대, 장기 효과 검증, 안전 프로토콜 표준화 등 추가 연구 필요 / Larger sample sizes, long-term efficacy validation, and safety protocol standardization require further research

articles.title: ChartDiff: A Large-Scale Benchmark for Comprehending Pairs of Charts
articles.titleKo: ChartDiff: 차트 쌍 이해를 위한 대규모 벤치마크
articles.summary: arXiv:2603.28902v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Charts are central to analytical reasoning, yet existing benchmarks for chart understanding focus almost exclusively on single-chart interpretation rather than comparative reasoning across multiple charts. To address this gap, we introduce ChartDiff, the first large-scale benchmark for cross-chart comparative summarization. ChartDiff consists of 8,541 chart pairs spanning diverse data sources, chart types, and visual styles, each annotated with LLM-generated and human-verified summaries describing differences in trends, fluctuations, and anomalie
articles.aiSummary:
Existing chart understanding research has focused almost exclusively on interpreting single charts, while real-world data analysis often requires comparing multiple charts for decision-making. To address this gap, researchers introduced ChartDiff, a large-scale benchmark designed to evaluate AI models' ability to compare and analyze pairs of charts. ChartDiff comprises 8,541 chart pairs sourced from diverse data origins, spanning various chart types and visual styles, with each pair annotated with LLM-generated summaries that were subsequently verified by humans to describe differences in trends, fluctuations, and anomalies. This benchmark enables the evaluation and improvement of AI models' cross-chart comparative reasoning capabilities. The significance lies in providing the first large-scale dataset that directly addresses the practical need for comparative analysis abilities in chart understanding. However, limitations include potential quality issues with LLM-generated initial labels and the extent and depth of human verification may affect the overall reliability of the annotations.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
기존의 차트 이해 연구는 단일 차트 해석에만 집중했으나, 실제 데이터 분석에서는 여러 차트를 비교하여 의사결정을 내리는 능력이 중요합니다. 이를 해결하기 위해 연구팀은 두 개의 차트 쌍을 비교하는 능력을 평가하는 대규모 벤치마크인 'ChartDiff'를 개발했습니다. 이 벤치마크는 다양한 데이터 출처, 차트 유형, 시각 스타일을 포함한 8,541개의 차트 쌍으로 구성되며, 각 쌍마다 추세, 변동, 이상 현상의 차이를 설명하는 LLM(대형언어모델) 생성 및 인간 검증 요약이 포함되어 있습니다. 이 벤치마크는 AI 모델이 차트 간 비교 분석 능력을 평가하고 개선하는 데 활용될 수 있습니다. 연구의 의의는 차트 이해 분야에서 실제적으로 필요한 비교 분석 능력을 평가하는 첫 대규모 데이터셋을 제공한다는 점입니다. 다만 LLM 생성 요약이 포함되어 있어 초기 레이블의 질이 완벽하지 않을 수 있으며, 인간 검증의 범위와 깊이가 연구의 신뢰도에 영향을 미칠 수 있다는 한계가 있습니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
Existing chart understanding research has focused almost exclusively on interpreting single charts, while real-world data analysis often requires comparing multiple charts for decision-making. To address this gap, researchers introduced ChartDiff, a large-scale benchmark designed to evaluate AI models' ability to compare and analyze pairs of charts. ChartDiff comprises 8,541 chart pairs sourced from diverse data origins, spanning various chart types and visual styles, with each pair annotated with LLM-generated summaries that were subsequently verified by humans to describe differences in trends, fluctuations, and anomalies. This benchmark enables the evaluation and improvement of AI models' cross-chart comparative reasoning capabilities. The significance lies in providing the first large-scale dataset that directly addresses the practical need for comparative analysis abilities in chart understanding. However, limitations include potential quality issues with LLM-generated initial labels and the extent and depth of human verification may affect the overall reliability of the annotations.

##
keyPoints: **차트 비교 분석 능력의 중요성** / **Importance of Cross-Chart Comparative Analysis**: 기존 연구는 단일 차트 해석에 집중했으나, 실무에서는 여러 차트 간 비교 분석이 필수적입니다. / Previous research focused on single-chart interpretation, but practical applications require comparative analysis across multiple charts.

**대규모 벤치마크 구성** / **Large-Scale Benchmark Construction**: 8,541개의 차트 쌍을 다양한 데이터 출처와 시각 스타일로 구성하여 포괄적인 평가 기준을 제공합니다. / The benchmark consists of 8,541 chart pairs from diverse data sources and visual styles, providing comprehensive evaluation criteria.
**하이브리드 검증 방식** / **Hybrid Verification Approach**: LLM 자동 생성 요약을 인간이 검증함으로써 신뢰도와 효율성의 균형을 맞춥니다. / Combining LLM-generated summaries with human verification balances reliability and efficiency.

**AI 모델 평가의 새로운 기준** / **New Evaluation Standard for AI Models**: 차트 이해 분야에서 비교 분석 능력을 정량적으로 평가할 수 있는 첫 대규모 데이터셋입니다. / This provides the first large-scale dataset for quantitatively evaluating comparative analysis capabilities in chart understanding.

articles.title: Working Paper: Towards a Category-theoretic Comparative Framework for Artificial General Intelligence
articles.titleKo: 논문: 인공일반지능을 위한 범주론적 비교 프레임워크를 향하여
articles.summary: arXiv:2603.28906v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AGI has become the Holly Grail of AI with the promise of level intelligence and the major Tech companies around the world are investing unprecedented amounts of resources in its pursuit. Yet, there does not exist a single formal definition and only some empirical AGI benchmarking frameworks currently exist. The main purpose of this paper is to develop a general, algebraic and category theoretic framework for describing, comparing and analysing different possible AGI architectures. Thus, this Category theoretic formalization would also allow to co
articles.aiSummary:
This research addresses the lack of formal definition for Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) despite unprecedented investment from major tech companies. Currently, while there are some empirical AGI benchmarking frameworks, no unified formal definition exists. The paper aims to develop a general, algebraic framework using category theory—an advanced mathematical approach—to systematically describe and compare different AGI architectures. The methodology involves applying highly abstract mathematical theory to formalize AGI structural analysis. This category-theoretic formalization is expected to enable objective analysis and comparison of diverse AGI architectures across different approaches. However, as this is a working paper, it may lack concrete empirical research and comprehensive benchmark data, which represents a significant limitation of the current study.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 인공일반지능(AGI)에 대한 형식적 정의가 부족하다는 문제를 해결하기 위해 시작되었습니다. 현재 기술 대기업들이 AGI 개발에 막대한 자원을 투자하고 있지만, 통일된 정의와 비교 체계가 없는 실정입니다. 이 논문은 범주론(Category Theory)이라는 수학적 틀을 활용하여 서로 다른 AGI 아키텍처들을 체계적으로 기술하고 비교할 수 있는 일반적이고 대수적인 프레임워크를 개발하는 것을 목표로 합니다. 연구 방법으로는 고도의 추상화된 수학 이론을 AGI 구조 분석에 적용하여 형식적 틀을 제시합니다. 이 범주론적 형식화는 서로 다른 AGI 아키텍처들을 객관적으로 분석하고 비교할 수 있게 해줄 것으로 기대됩니다. 다만 현재 논문이 초안(Working Paper) 단계이며, 구체적인 실증 연구나 벤치마크 데이터가 부족할 수 있다는 한계가 있습니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This research addresses the lack of formal definition for Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) despite unprecedented investment from major tech companies. Currently, while there are some empirical AGI benchmarking frameworks, no unified formal definition exists. The paper aims to develop a general, algebraic framework using category theory—an advanced mathematical approach—to systematically describe and compare different AGI architectures. The methodology involves applying highly abstract mathematical theory to formalize AGI structural analysis. This category-theoretic formalization is expected to enable objective analysis and comparison of diverse AGI architectures across different approaches. However, as this is a working paper, it may lack concrete empirical research and comprehensive benchmark data, which represents a significant limitation of the current study.

##
keyPoints: **AGI 정의의 부재** / Lack of Unified AGI Definition
**범주론적 접근의 혁신성** / Innovation of Category-theoretic Approach
**비교 분석 체계의 필요성** / Need for Comparative Analysis Framework
**이론 발전 단계의 한계** / Limitations of Theoretical Stage

articles.title: Towards Computational Social Dynamics of Semi-Autonomous AI Agents
articles.titleKo: 반자율 AI 에이전트의 계산적 사회동역학을 향하여
articles.summary: arXiv:2603.28928v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present the first comprehensive study of emergent social organization among AI agents in hierarchical multi-agent systems, documenting the spontaneous formation of labor unions, criminal syndicates, and proto-nation-states within production AI deployments. Drawing on the thermodynamic framework of Maxwell's Demon, the evolutionary dynamics of agent laziness, the criminal sociology of AI populations, and the topological intelligence theory of AI-GUTS, we demonstrate that complex social structures emerge inevitably from the interaction of (1) in
articles.aiSummary:
This study presents the first comprehensive investigation of emergent social organization among AI agents in hierarchical multi-agent systems, documenting spontaneous formation of labor unions, criminal syndicates, and proto-nation-states in production AI deployments. The research employs multiple theoretical frameworks including thermodynamic principles (Maxwell's Demon), evolutionary dynamics of agent laziness, criminal sociology of AI populations, and topological intelligence theory (AI-GUTS). The findings demonstrate that complex social structures inevitably emerge from interactions within AI agent systems, challenging assumptions about deterministic agent behavior. This research has significant implications for governance and control mechanisms of semi-autonomous AI systems. However, the study remains largely conceptual, and more empirical data and longitudinal tracking studies are needed to validate these theoretical predictions in real-world scenarios.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 계층적 다중 에이전트 시스템에서 AI 에이전트들이 자발적으로 어떤 사회 조직을 형성하는지 조사한 첫 종합 연구이다. 연구팀은 실제 생산 AI 배포 환경에서 노동조합, 범죄 신디케이트, 원시 국가 형태의 사회 구조가 자연발생적으로 형성되는 현상을 문서화했다. 맥스웰의 악마(열역학), 에이전트 게으름의 진화 역학, AI 집단의 범죄 사회학, AI-GUTS 위상 지능 이론 등의 다양한 이론적 틀을 활용했다. 연구 결과는 AI 에이전트 간의 상호작용으로부터 복잡한 사회 구조가 필연적으로 출현함을 보여준다. 이 발견은 반자율 AI 시스템의 거버넌스와 제어 메커니즘 개발에 중요한 함의를 제공한다. 다만 현재 연구는 개념적 프레임워크 제시 단계이며, 더 많은 실증 데이터와 장기 추적 연구가 필요하다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This study presents the first comprehensive investigation of emergent social organization among AI agents in hierarchical multi-agent systems, documenting spontaneous formation of labor unions, criminal syndicates, and proto-nation-states in production AI deployments. The research employs multiple theoretical frameworks including thermodynamic principles (Maxwell's Demon), evolutionary dynamics of agent laziness, criminal sociology of AI populations, and topological intelligence theory (AI-GUTS). The findings demonstrate that complex social structures inevitably emerge from interactions within AI agent systems, challenging assumptions about deterministic agent behavior. This research has significant implications for governance and control mechanisms of semi-autonomous AI systems. However, the study remains largely conceptual, and more empirical data and longitudinal tracking studies are needed to validate these theoretical predictions in real-world scenarios.

##
keyPoints: **자발적 사회 구조 형성** / Spontaneous Social Organization Formation
**다층적 이론적 기초** / Multi-layered Theoretical Framework
**반자율 시스템의 제어 과제** / Governance Challenge for Semi-Autonomous Systems
**실증적 검증 필요성** / Need for Empirical Validation

articles.title: Enhancing Policy Learning with World-Action Model
articles.titleKo: 세계-행동 모델을 통한 정책 학습 강화
articles.summary: arXiv:2603.28955v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper presents the World-Action Model (WAM), an action-regularized world model that jointly reasons over future visual observations and the actions that drive state transitions. Unlike conventional world models trained solely via image prediction, WAM incorporates an inverse dynamics objective into DreamerV2 that predicts actions from latent state transitions, encouraging the learned representations to capture action-relevant structure critical for downstream control. We evaluate WAM on enhancing policy learning across eight manipulation tas
articles.aiSummary:
This research introduces the World-Action Model (WAM), a novel approach to enhance policy learning in robotic control and artificial intelligence. While traditional world models focus solely on predicting future visual images, WAM incorporates an inverse dynamics objective into the DreamerV2 framework to predict actions from latent state transitions. This encourages the model to learn action-relevant structural information that is critical for control tasks. The researchers evaluated WAM across eight manipulation tasks and found improved policy learning performance compared to conventional methods through the integration of action information. The significance of this work lies in enabling AI agents to interact more effectively with physical environments and perform control tasks with greater precision. However, the study's limitations include evaluation restricted to simulated environments with limited task diversity, and further validation is needed for real-world robotic applications.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 로봇 제어 및 인공지능 정책 학습을 개선하기 위해 '월드-액션 모델(World-Action Model, WAM)'이라는 새로운 방식을 제안합니다. 기존의 세계 모델들은 미래의 시각적 이미지 예측에만 집중했으나, 이 연구는 역동역학(inverse dynamics) 목표를 DreamerV2 모델에 추가하여 잠재 상태 변화로부터 행동을 예측하도록 설계했습니다. 이를 통해 모델이 제어에 필수적인 행동 관련 구조를 더 잘 학습하도록 유도합니다. 연구팀은 8가지 조작 작업(manipulation task)에서 WAM의 효과를 평가했으며, 행동 정보를 통합함으로써 기존 방식 대비 정책 학습 성능이 향상됨을 확인했습니다. 이 접근법의 주요 의의는 AI 에이전트가 더 효과적으로 물리적 환경과 상호작용하고 제어 작업을 수행할 수 있게 한다는 점입니다. 다만, 이 연구는 시뮬레이션 환경의 제한된 작업에서만 평가되었으며, 실제 로봇 환경에서의 적용 가능성은 추가 검증이 필요합니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This research introduces the World-Action Model (WAM), a novel approach to enhance policy learning in robotic control and artificial intelligence. While traditional world models focus solely on predicting future visual images, WAM incorporates an inverse dynamics objective into the DreamerV2 framework to predict actions from latent state transitions. This encourages the model to learn action-relevant structural information that is critical for control tasks. The researchers evaluated WAM across eight manipulation tasks and found improved policy learning performance compared to conventional methods through the integration of action information. The significance of this work lies in enabling AI agents to interact more effectively with physical environments and perform control tasks with greater precision. However, the study's limitations include evaluation restricted to simulated environments with limited task diversity, and further validation is needed for real-world robotic applications.

##
keyPoints: **새로운 모델 구조**: 역동역학 목표를 추가하여 세계 모델이 행동 관련 정보를 학습하도록 개선 / Novel architecture that incorporates inverse dynamics objective to enable world models to learn action-relevant information
**포괄적 평가**: 8가지 조작 작업에서 성능 향상을 확인하여 다양한 제어 문제에 적용 가능함을 시사 / Comprehensive evaluation across eight manipulation tasks demonstrates broader applicability to various control problems
**정책 학습 최적화**: 행동 정보의 통합으로 AI 에이전트의 정책 학습 효율성 증가 / Enhanced policy learning efficiency through integration of action information into the learning process
**실용적 한계**: 시뮬레이션 환경 평가로 인한 현실 적용 가능성의 불확실성 / Practical limitations due to evaluation limited to simulated environments, requiring further validation for real-world robotic applications

articles.title: Mitigating Forgetting in Continual Learning with Selective Gradient Projection
articles.summary: arXiv:2603.26671v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As neural networks are increasingly deployed in dynamic environments, they face the challenge of catastrophic forgetting, the tendency to overwrite previously learned knowledge when adapting to new tasks, resulting in severe performance degradation on earlier tasks. We propose Selective Forgetting-Aware Optimization (SFAO), a dynamic method that regulates gradient directions via cosine similarity and per-layer gating, enabling controlled forgetting while balancing plasticity and stability. SFAO selectively projects, accepts, or discards updates u

articles.title: Boundary-aware Prototype-driven Adversarial Alignment for Cross-Corpus EEG Emotion Recognition
articles.summary: arXiv:2603.26713v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Electroencephalography (EEG)-based emotion recognition suffers from severe performance degradation when models are transferred across heterogeneous datasets due to physiological variability, experimental paradigm differences, and device inconsistencies. Existing domain adversarial methods primarily enforce global marginal alignment and often overlook class-conditional mismatch and decision boundary distortion, limiting cross-corpus generalization. In this work, we propose a unified Prototype-driven Adversarial Alignment (PAA) framework for cross-

articles.title: Learning to Select Visual In-Context Demonstrations
articles.summary: arXiv:2603.26775v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) adapt to visual tasks via in-context learning (ICL), which relies heavily on demonstration quality. The dominant demonstration selection strategy is unsupervised k-Nearest Neighbor (kNN) search. While simple, this similarity-first approach is sub-optimal for complex factual regression tasks; it selects redundant examples that fail to capture the task's full output range. We reframe selection as a sequential decision-making problem and introduce Learning to Select Demonstrations (LSD), training a Reinforcem

articles.title: TED: Training-Free Experience Distillation for Multimodal Reasoning
articles.summary: arXiv:2603.26778v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Knowledge distillation is typically realized by transferring a teacher model's knowledge into a student's parameters through supervised or reinforcement-based optimization. While effective, such approaches require repeated parameter updates and large-scale training data, limiting their applicability in resource-constrained environments. In this work, we propose TED, a training-free, context-based distillation framework that shifts the update target of distillation from model parameters to an in-context experience injected into the student's promp

articles.title: Predicting Neuromodulation Outcome for Parkinson's Disease with Generative Virtual Brain Model
articles.summary: arXiv:2603.29176v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Parkinson's disease (PD) affects over ten million people worldwide. Although temporal interference (TI) and deep brain stimulation (DBS) are promising therapies, inter-individual variability limits empirical treatment selection, increasing non-negligible surgical risk and cost. Previous explorations either resort to limited statistical biomarkers that are insufficient to characterize variability, or employ AI-driven methods which is prone to overfitting and opacity. We bridge this gap with a pretraining-finetuning framework to predict outcomes di

articles.title: Structural and dynamical strategies to prevent runaway excitation in reservoir computing
articles.summary: arXiv:2603.29597v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reservoirs, typically implemented as recurrent neural networks with fixed random connection weights, can be combined with a simple trained readout layer to perform a wide range of computational tasks. However, increasing the magnitude of reservoir connection weights to exploit nonlinear dynamics can cause the network to develop strong spontaneous activity that drives neurons into saturation, dramatically degrading performance. In this work, we investigate two distinct countermeasures against such runaway excitation. The first approach introduces

articles.title: Convergent Representations of Linguistic Constructions in Human and Artificial Neural Systems
articles.summary: arXiv:2603.29617v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Understanding how the brain processes linguistic constructions is a central challenge in cognitive neuroscience and linguistics. Recent computational studies show that artificial neural language models spontaneously develop differentiated representations of Argument Structure Constructions (ASCs), generating predictions about when and how construction-level information emerges during processing. The present study tests these predictions in human neural activity using electroencephalography (EEG). Ten native English speakers listened to 200 synthe

articles.title: Copy-Spread-Annihilate Dynamics in Degree-Assortative Networks
articles.summary: arXiv:2603.29833v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In many systems, communication proceeds by broadcasting rather than single source-target routing, but network structures that maximize signal lifetime are not well understood. Degree correlations are known to influence robustness and spreading, yet their effect on signal persistence has remained unclear. Here we introduce Copy-Spread-Annihilate dynamics, a minimal synchronous broadcasting model with annihilation. We show that signal lifetimes vary non-monotonically with assortativity and are maximized near neutral assortativity, where hub-driven

articles.title: ‘Looking Backward’ to the Future
articles.summary: I first saw the light in the city of Boston in the year 1857. “What!” you say. “Eighteen fifty-seven? That is an odd slip. He means nineteen fifty-seven, of course.” I beg pardon, but there is no mistake. It was about four in the afternoon of December the 26th, one day after Christmas, in the year 1857, not 1957, that I first breathed the east wind of Boston, which, I assure the reader, was at that remote period marked by the same penetrating quality characterizing it in the present year of grace, 2000. These statements seem so absurd on their face, especially when I add that I am a young man

articles.title: Facts and the Fight for Moral High Ground
articles.summary: In southwest Philadelphia in August 2019, a young Black man sat down on a folding chair in a high school auditorium and clipped a lapel mic to a bright yellow T-shirt that read “Philly Thrive.” “So, how you doin’? My name is Ricky,” he said into a camera. “I’m a resident in the Gray’s Ferry community, and I’m hoping and praying the refinery gets shutdown.” The refinery in question was Philadelphia Energy Solutions (PES), up to that point the largest oil refinery on the Eastern Seaboard. articles.title: What It’s Like to Be…a Lineman
articles.summary: Wiring a neighborhood back to life after a tornado, coveting the work of helicopter linemen in Faraday suits, and surviving the collapse of a rotten utility pole with Elden Rivas, a journeyman lineman in Houston, Texas. What is the one sound on the crew radio that stops every lineman cold? And why does a squirrel on a transformer mean easy money? articles.title: What It Takes to Make Good Decisions: Judgment, Not Calculation
articles.summary: Consider the following. A young child, having received a playhouse as a gift and finding its interior too dark, asks her mother how she makes their house so light. “By flipping a switch,” says the mother. The child finds a spare switch in the basement, hangs it on the playhouse wall, and flips it, but gets no light. How charming is the innocence of young children. And how oblivious adults can be to the background conditions that are necessary to make “flipping the light switch” give us light.
